report_name: DragonForce Ransomware Gang Claims Attack on Taos Mountain Casino, Stealing SSNs

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that conducted a double-extortion attack against Taos Mountain Casino, exfiltrating 38.63 GB of sensitive data, including PII and SSNs, and subsequently encrypting network systems. The group claimed the attack on their dark web leak site.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: DragonForce ransomware was used to encrypt files on the Taos Mountain Casino's network to disrupt operations as part of a double-extortion scheme.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: The DragonForce group exfiltrated 38.63 GB of data to a location under their control before deploying ransomware, a technique similar to Transfer Data to Cloud Account.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors likely deleted volume shadow copies using tools like vssadmin to prevent easy system recovery from backups, a common tactic for ransomware groups.

malware.name: DragonForce Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the DragonForce group to encrypt systems at Taos Mountain Casino.

identities.name: Taos Mountain Casino
identities.description: A gaming establishment in Taos, New Mexico, that was the victim of a DragonForce ransomware attack, resulting in a data breach of PII and Social Security numbers.

identities.name: Kroll
identities.description: A third-party firm engaged by Taos Mountain Casino to provide credit monitoring and identity restoration services to victims of the data breach.

infrastructure.name: DragonForce Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the DragonForce ransomware group to publish claims of attacks and leak stolen data to extort victims.

infrastructure.name: DragonForce Data Exfiltration Destination
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled location where 38.63 GB of sensitive data from Taos Mountain Casino was transferred before the ransomware was deployed.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e6f-4a3b-8c1d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware deployment to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/taos-mountain-casino-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic to inhibit system recovery, as suggested by the article's detection guidance.

indicators.name: Common Ransomware Note Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Common Ransomware Note Creation
id: 8c1d9e8f-7a6b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with common ransom note names, such as README.txt, which often accompanies ransomware encryption.
references:
  - https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/taos-mountain-casino-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\README.txt'
  filter_legit_paths:
    TargetFilename|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_paths
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files with common ransom note names, such as README.txt, which often accompanies ransomware encryption activities.
